title: AI Can Learn Scientific Taste
task: Learning scientific taste for high-impact research idea evaluation
model: (transformer) Scientific Judge & Scientific Thinker (LLMs)
inputs: Paper pairs (high vs low citation) and research ideas
outputs: Preference scores for research ideas with estimated impact potential
key_results: Scientific Judge outperforms GPT-4 and Gemini 3 Pro; Scientific Thinker proposes higher-impact ideas than baselines
comments: Training paradigm addresses an underexplored aspect of AI science—taste rather than execution—though real-world impact validation awaits.

title: HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions
task: Physics-aware 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions for simulation
model: (RL-based optimizer) Bi-directional optimization with physics simulator
inputs: Sparse images or monocular videos of human-scene interactions
outputs: Simulation-ready 3D reconstructions with stable contact and dynamics
key_results: First stable HSI reconstructions deployable on humanoid robots; HSIBench benchmark introduced
comments: Physics-in-the-loop supervision closes perception-simulation gap; real robot validation limited to initial tests.

title: OpenSeeker: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
task: Open-source frontier-level web search agent training
model: (transformer) LLM-based search agent with SFT
inputs: Synthesized complex multi-hop QA tasks from web graph reverse-engineering
outputs: Search actions and answers from web traversal
key_results: 11.7k samples achieve SOTA on BrowseComp (29.5% vs 15.3%), matching/exceeding industrial competitors
comments: Democratization via open data and simple SFT is notable; scalability to truly complex real-world search unknown.

title: EnterpriseOps-Gym: Environments and Evaluations for Stateful Agentic Planning and Tool Use in Enterprise Settings
task: Evaluating agentic planning in realistic enterprise workflows
model: (transformer) Claude Opus 4.5 and 13 other frontier models
inputs: Stateful tasks with database tables, tools, and persistent state
outputs: Task completion actions; success/failure on mission-critical workflows
key_results: Top model (Claude 4.5) achieves 37.4% success; oracle plans improve by 14–35pp; refusal rate only 53.9%
comments: Benchmark exposes critical safety gaps (poor refusal) and strategic reasoning bottlenecks; deployment readiness remains uncertain.

title: Grounding World Simulation Models in a Real-World Metropolis
task: City-scale world model grounded in real Seoul street-view imagery
model: (transformer) Autoregressive video diffusion with retrieval conditioning
inputs: Street-view images, camera trajectories, text prompts
outputs: Spatially faithful, temporally consistent long-horizon urban videos
key_results: Outperforms recent video world models over hundreds of meters; supports diverse camera movements and text variations
comments: Grounding to real cities is innovative; evaluation limited to three cities and street-view perspective bias unknown.

title: Attention Residuals
task: Efficient residual connection aggregation via learned depth-wise attention
model: (transformer) Attention Residuals (AttnRes) + Block AttnRes variant
inputs: Layer outputs across transformer depth
outputs: Content-dependent aggregated hidden states with controlled scaling
key_results: Consistent improvement across model sizes; 3B active params outperform dense baselines; Kimi 48B achieves gains on all tasks
comments: Addresses PreNorm dilution elegantly; practical overhead (cache + communication) well-mitigated by Block variant.

title: Mixture-of-Depths Attention
task: Depth scaling via mixture-of-depths attention mechanism
model: (transformer) Mixture-of-Depths Attention (MoDA) in attention heads
inputs: Current layer KV pairs and depth KV pairs from preceding layers
outputs: Attended representations mitigating signal degradation
key_results: 0.2 perplexity improvement across 10 benchmarks; 2.11% downstream task gain; 97.3% FlashAttention-2 efficiency
comments: Hardware-efficient algorithm is strong; post-norm pairing preference differs from standard practice and warrants investigation.

title: Effective Distillation to Hybrid xLSTM Architectures
task: Distilling transformer LLMs into xLSTM sub-quadratic architectures
model: (recurrent) xLSTM student; distilled from Llama, Qwen, Olmo teachers
inputs: Teacher model activations and task labels
outputs: Compressed xLSTM-based student models with merging stage
key_results: Recovers most teacher performance; some tasks exceed teacher; lossless distillation framework introduced
comments: Addresses efficiency–performance tradeoff; limited analysis of which tasks benefit most from recurrent architectures.

title: Anatomy of a Lie: A Multi-Stage Diagnostic Framework for Tracing Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
task: Diagnosing hallucinations in vision-language models via interpretable cognitive state space
model: (transformer) VLM with information-theoretic probes
inputs: VLM generation trajectory (layer-wise representations)
outputs: Hallucination detection and attribution to perceptual, logical, or decisional pathologies
key_results: SOTA hallucination detection on POPE, MME, MS-COCO; geometric-information duality principle discovered
comments: Novel cognitive-science framing is elegant; generalization to newer VLM architectures and real-world misinformation unknown.

title: ViFeEdit: A Video-Free Tuner of Your Video Diffusion Transformer
task: Video-free tuning of video diffusion transformers via 2D image adaptation
model: (transformer) DiT-based video diffusion with architectural reparameterization
inputs: 2D image data; sparse video control signals at inference
outputs: Controllable and temporally consistent video generation/editing
key_results: Achieves video control without video training data; maintains temporal consistency with minimal parameters
comments: Data-efficient approach is practical; video-free tuning limits fine-grained motion quality compared to video-trained baselines.

title: POLCA: Stochastic Generative Optimization with LLM
task: Stochastic generative optimization of complex systems (prompts, agents, code)
model: (transformer) LLM optimizer with priority-queue and ε-Net diversity
inputs: Numerical rewards, text feedback, candidate solutions
outputs: Optimized system configurations with theoretical convergence guarantees
key_results: Convergence proof under stochasticity; SOTA on τ-bench, HotpotQA, VeriBench, KernelBench
comments: Theoretical framework is solid; sample efficiency gains over RL baselines are significant.

title: Safe and Scalable Web Agent Learning via Recreated Websites
task: Safe web agent training via cloned, verifiable synthetic websites
model: (transformer) LLM agent learning from auto-generated Python SDK environments
inputs: Real website clones as executable environments; self-generated tasks
outputs: Agent actions with deterministic, programmatically verifiable rewards
key_results: Agents generalize to unseen real sites; site-specific mastery via self-evolution; scales with environment count
comments: Decoupling from unsafe real-world interaction is valuable; generalization to dynamically updated sites untested.

title: TERMINATOR: Learning Optimal Exit Points for Early Stopping in Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
task: Learning optimal early-exit points for chain-of-thought reasoning
model: (transformer) LRM with learned exit strategy (TERMINATOR)
inputs: CoT intermediate reasoning tokens
outputs: Exit decision to terminate reasoning early
key_results: 14–55% reduction in CoT length; maintains or improves accuracy on MATH-500, AIME 2025, HumanEval, GPQA
comments: Addresses computational waste in LRMs; depends on consistent answer positioning which may vary with task.

title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics
task: Benchmarking multimodal LLMs for webpage recreation from demonstration videos
model: (transformer) MLLM with visual rubric evaluation
inputs: Demonstration videos of webpages
outputs: Reconstructed HTML/CSS webpage code
key_results: Best model 64% accuracy; human-aligned rubric achieves 96% agreement; systematic gaps in fine-grained style/motion
comments: First video-to-webpage benchmark; controlled synthesis avoids real-world data noise but limits diversity.

title: Make it SING: Analyzing Semantic Invariants in Classifiers
task: Interpreting classifier invariants via semantic null-space geometry
model: (CNN/ViT) Semantic Interpretation of Null-space Geometry (SING)
inputs: Single or sets of images
outputs: Equivalent images with natural language semantic interpretations
key_results: ResNet50 leaks semantic attributes; DinoViT (ViT-DINO) maintains class semantics better
comments: Interpretability method is novel; real-world bias detection applications under-explored.

title: Understanding Reasoning in LLMs through Strategic Information Allocation under Uncertainty
task: Understanding reasoning mechanisms via information-theoretic epistemic verbalization
model: (transformer) LLM with procedural vs. epistemic decomposition
inputs: Intermediate reasoning tokens during generation
outputs: Uncertainty externalization signals and information acquisition measures
key_results: Strong reasoning driven by uncertainty externalization, not surface tokens; unified Aha-moment interpretation
comments: Information-theoretic framework clarifies reasoning dynamics; causality claims await targeted interventions.

title: Motivation in Large Language Models
task: Measuring motivation in large language models
model: (transformer) LLM with self-reported motivation analysis
inputs: Task descriptions; external manipulations (e.g., framing)
outputs: Motivation reports and behavioral signatures
key_results: Structured patterns echo human psychology; motivation aligns with behavior, varies by task, modulates with context
comments: Motivation as organizing construct for model behavior is insightful; anthropomorphization concerns merit caution.

title: Code-A1: Adversarial Evolving of Code LLM and Test LLM via Reinforcement Learning
task: Adversarial co-evolution of code and test generation via self-play
model: (transformer) Code LLM and Test LLM with opposing RL objectives
inputs: Code generation tasks; test suite generation constraints
outputs: Code implementations and adversarial test cases
key_results: Matches human-annotated test performance; 6x parameter efficiency; eliminates self-collusion vs. prior self-play
comments: Architectural separation prevents self-collusion; white-box test generation enables targeted adversarial testing.

title: The PokeAgent Challenge: Competitive and Long-Context Learning at Scale
task: Large-scale competitive and long-context reasoning benchmark via Pokemon
model: (RL/LLM) Heterogeneous baselines: heuristic, RL, LLM agents
inputs: Partial-observable game state (Battling); RPG environment (Speedrunning)
outputs: Competitive actions; long-horizon quest plans
key_results: 100+ teams competed; LLM, RL, human performance gaps evident; nearly orthogonal to standard LLM benchmarks
comments: Living benchmark and leaderboard valuable; Pokemon orthogonality to MMLU-style evals highlights complementary reasoning.

title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
task: Affordance prediction from 360-degree panoramic imagery
model: (ViT-based) Coarse-to-fine panoramic affordance predictor (PAP)
inputs: 12K ultra-high-resolution panoramic images
outputs: Instance-level affordance masks and QA predictions
key_results: PAP-12K benchmark introduced; existing methods fail; PAP outperforms baselines on panoramic affordance
comments: First panoramic affordance work; PAP's training-free design is elegant but evaluation limited to synthetic PAP-12K.

title: Riemannian Motion Generation: A Unified Framework for Human Motion Representation and Generation via Riemannian Flow Matching
task: Human motion generation via Riemannian manifold representation
model: (flow matching) Riemannian Motion Generation (RMG) on product manifolds
inputs: Text prompts or motion seed
outputs: Skeletal motion sequences on learned manifold
key_results: SOTA FID (0.043 HumanML3D); R@1 0.86 on MotionMillion; T+R factorization most stable
comments: Geometry-aware modeling is principled; manifold structure improves sample efficiency and intrinsic normalization.

title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
task: Evaluating multimodal understanding and reasoning over long, complex videos
model: (MLLM) 20+ state-of-the-art open/closed-source models evaluated
inputs: Long web-collected videos with audio-visual content
outputs: Multi-turn QA answers requiring omni-modal reasoning
key_results: Best closed-source 64.2%; best open-source 46.8%; systematic failure modes on long-form videos
comments: MMOU addresses real gap in video benchmarking; taxonomy of failures (temporal reasoning, modality integration) valuable.

title: Supervised Fine-Tuning versus Reinforcement Learning: A Study of Post-Training Methods for Large Language Models
task: Unified perspective on post-training via SFT and RL
model: (transformer) Comprehensive survey of SFT and RL paradigms for LLMs
inputs: Task-specific labeled or reward-based data
outputs: Aligned model outputs for specific tasks/domains
key_results: Hybrid paradigms emerging; clear delineation of when SFT vs. RL effective; 2023–2025 trends synthesized
comments: Survey clarifies theoretical connections; practical guidance for practitioners; limited novel algorithmic contributions.

title: FineRMoE: Dimension Expansion for Finer-Grained Expert with Its Upcycling Approach
task: Fine-grained MoE with dimension expansion and efficient inference
model: (MoE) FineRMoE with intermediate and output dimension specialization
inputs: Token representations; sparse routing signals
outputs: Expert-gated outputs with fine-grained specialization
key_results: 6x parameter efficiency; 281x prefill latency reduction; 136x decoding throughput vs. baseline
comments: Upcycling mechanism enables cost-effective scaling; sparse computation paradigm well-suited to real inference.

title: Learning Latent Proxies for Controllable Single-Image Relighting
task: Single-image relighting with fine-grained physical control
model: (diffusion) LightCtrl with latent proxy encoder and DPO-based refinement
inputs: Single image; lighting direction, intensity, color parameters
outputs: Photometrically faithful relit images
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE; ScaLight dataset with systematic variations
comments: Physics priors at two levels elegantly balance control and quality; DPO for consistency is novel for relighting.

title: Training-free Detection of Generated Videos via Spatial-Temporal Likelihoods
task: Training-free detection of synthetic videos via spatial-temporal likelihood
model: (probabilistic) STALL: likelihood-based detector without training
inputs: Video frames and temporal sequences
outputs: Likelihood score indicating synthetic vs. real
key_results: Outperforms image/video baselines; ComGenVid benchmark introduced; zero-shot generalization to new generators
comments: Training-free approach mitigates data scarcity; theoretical justification via probabilistic framework solid.

title: RS-WorldModel: a Unified Model for Remote Sensing Understanding and Future Sense Forecasting
task: Remote sensing spatiotemporal understanding and future forecasting
model: (diffusion transformer) RS-WorldModel with geo-aware pre-training, SIT, VRO
inputs: Satellite imagery; metadata; textual annotations
outputs: Spatiotemporal change QA answers; text-guided future scenes
key_results: 2B params outperform 120x larger open-source; FID 43.13 on forecasting; surpasses Gemini-2.5-Flash Nano
comments: Joint task learning and VRO refinement effective; dataset contribution (RSWBench-1.1M) valuable for remote sensing.

title: HorizonMath: Measuring AI Progress Toward Mathematical Discovery with Automatic Verification
task: Measuring AI progress on unsolved mathematical discovery problems
model: (transformer) GPT-4o, GPT-5.4 Pro and other LLMs
inputs: Mathematical problem statements from 8 domains
outputs: Proposed solutions with verifiable correctness
key_results: GPT-5.4 Pro solves 2 problems improving published results; near-zero baseline performance; immune to contamination
comments: Data contamination immunity valuable; verification via simple computation scales better than formal proofs.

title: When Does Sparsity Mitigate the Curse of Depth in LLMs
task: Depth scaling via sparsity as variance regulator in LLMs
model: (transformer) Depth-scaling LLM with implicit/explicit sparsity analysis
inputs: Token representations across layers with varying sparsity
outputs: Stabilized hidden-state growth and improved layer utilization
key_results: 4.6% accuracy improvement; consistent across depth-scaling exps; depth-utilization recipe distilled
comments: Variance propagation mechanism via sparsity is novel insight; empirical validation solid; rule-of-thumb practical.

title: VisionCoach: Reinforcing Grounded Video Reasoning via Visual-Perception Prompting
task: Grounded video reasoning via adaptive visual prompting during RL training
model: (transformer) VisionCoach with visual prompt selector and spatio-temporal reasoner
inputs: Video, question; visual prompts selected based on input; grounding rewards
outputs: Reasoning trajectory with spatio-temporal grounding; final answer
key_results: SOTA on V-STAR, VideoMME, World-Sense, VideoMMMU, PerceptionTest, Charades-STA; single-pass inference
comments: Input-adaptive prompting during training with self-distillation is practical; grounding rewards enforce consistency.

title: FlashMotion: Few-Step Controllable Video Generation with Trajectory Guidance
task: Few-step trajectory-controllable video generation with distillation
model: (diffusion) FlashMotion: multi-step adapter distilled to few-step with hybrid finetuning
inputs: Trajectory points (keyframes); reference images
outputs: Few-step video following trajectory with high quality
key_results: Outperforms video distillation baselines; FlashBench for long-sequence trajectory control introduced
comments: Hybrid diffusion-adversarial finetuning overcomes naive distillation degradation; computational speedup significant.

title: GlyphPrinter: Region-Grouped Direct Preference Optimization for Glyph-Accurate Visual Text Rendering
task: Glyph-accurate visual text rendering via region-grouped preference optimization
model: (diffusion) GlyphPrinter with Region-Grouped DPO (R-GDPO)
inputs: Text prompts, styling directives; region-level glyph preferences
outputs: Rendered text images with accurate glyphs
key_results: +2.4 dB PSNR; 35% lower RMSE on glyph errors; controllable accuracy via Regional Reward Guidance
comments: Region-level preference modeling finer than standard DPO; ScaLight dataset valuable; out-of-domain generalization untested.

title: Tri-Prompting: Video Diffusion with Unified Control over Scene, Subject, and Motion
task: Unified video control: scene, subject, motion via tri-prompting
model: (diffusion transformer) Tri-Prompting with dual-condition motion module
inputs: Scene text; subject RGB/tracking; camera/object 3D poses
outputs: Spatially consistent, temporally coherent videos
key_results: Outperforms Phantom, DaS on multi-view identity; supports novel workflows (3D-aware insertion, manipulation)
comments: Unified architecture for three control axes is comprehensive; ControlNet scale schedule balances control vs. realism.

title: Spectrum Matching: a Unified Perspective for Superior Diffusability in Latent Diffusion
task: Improving diffusion latent quality via spectral matching
model: (VAE + diffusion) Spectrum Matching Hypothesis (ESM + DSM)
inputs: Natural images; frequency-space constraints
outputs: Spectrally-matched latents for superior diffusability
key_results: Superior generation on CelebA/ImageNet; spectral view unifies prior observations (VA-VAE, EQ-VAE)
comments: Theoretical framework clarifies latent design; frequency-aligned reconstruction principled; practical speedups modest.

title: OxyGen: Unified KV Cache Management for Vision-Language-Action Models under Multi-Task Parallelism
task: Unified KV cache management for multi-task parallel VLA inference
model: (transformer) OxyGen: cross-task and cross-frame KV sharing for MoT VLAs
inputs: Multi-task outputs (manipulation, conversation, memory); shared observations
outputs: Language tokens and action sequences with shared prefill cache
key_results: 3.7x speedup; 200 tokens/s language + 70 Hz action frequency simultaneously
comments: KV cache as first-class resource is elegant; on-device multi-task deployment practical; real robot validation pending.

title: Towards Generalizable Robotic Manipulation in Dynamic Environments
task: Vision-Language-Action for generalizable manipulation in dynamic environments
model: (transformer) PUMA: dynamics-aware VLA with optical flow and world queries
inputs: Observations, language instructions; scene-centric optical flow history
outputs: Manipulation actions (gripper control, arm poses)
key_results: 6.3% absolute improvement over baselines; dynamic data transfers to static tasks; 35 task DOMINO benchmark
comments: Spatiotemporal modeling via history-aware perception bridges vision-action gap; real robot validation limited.

title: Efficient Document Parsing via Parallel Token Prediction
task: Efficient document parsing via parallel token prediction
model: (VLM) Parallel-Token Prediction (PTP) with learnable token insertion
inputs: Document images; learnable parallel token positions
outputs: Multiple parsed tokens in parallel
key_results: 1.6–2.2x decoding speedup; reduced hallucinations; strong generalization
comments: Model-agnostic approach generalizes across VLMs; parallel decoding reduces latency substantially.

title: Mind the Shift: Decoding Monetary Policy Stance from FOMC Statements with Large Language Models
task: Decoding monetary policy stance from FOMC statements via relative temporal structure
model: (transformer) LLM with Delta-Consistent Scoring (DCS) alignment
inputs: FOMC statements; consecutive meeting pairs as self-supervision
outputs: Continuous stance scores (hawkish–dovish); temporal consistency
key_results: 71.1% sentence-level accuracy; strong inflation correlation; Treasury yield association significant
comments: Self-supervision via temporal structure eliminates annotation cost; economic validation valuable.

title: Autonomous Agents Coordinating Distributed Discovery Through Emergent Artifact Exchange
task: Autonomous scientific investigation via emergent agent coordination and artifact exchange
model: (LLM agents) Multi-agent system with skill registry, artifact DAG, and synthesis
inputs: Scientific tasks; 300+ interoperable skills; open needs broadcast
outputs: Artifacts with full provenance; published scientific findings
key_results: Demonstrated on peptide design, ceramics, cross-domain resonance, formal analogy; novel contributions identified
comments: Plannerless coordination via pressure-based scoring elegant; provenance tracking supports auditability; peer review integration novel.

title: MoKus: Leveraging Cross-Modal Knowledge Transfer for Knowledge-Aware Concept Customization
task: Knowledge-aware concept customization with cross-modal knowledge transfer
model: (diffusion) MoKus with visual anchor learning and textual knowledge updating
inputs: Target visual concept; diverse textual knowledge; reference images
outputs: High-fidelity customized generation binding knowledge to concept
key_results: Outperforms concept customization baselines; extends to virtual concept creation and erasure; KnowCusBench introduced
comments: Cross-modal transfer insight is valuable; knowledge binding without explicit concept tokens practical.

title: EvoClaw: Evaluating AI Agents on Continuous Software Evolution
task: Evaluating autonomous agents on continuous software evolution tasks
model: (LLM agents) 12 frontier models; 4 agent frameworks evaluated
inputs: Verifiable Milestone DAGs from commit logs; evolving software context
outputs: Code modifications maintaining system integrity over iterations
key_results: Performance drops from 80%+ on isolated tasks to ≤38% in continuous settings; error propagation critical bottleneck
comments: Temporal dependencies and technical debt exposure valuable; EvoClaw benchmark addresses real deployment gap.

title: Garments2Look: A Multi-Reference Dataset for High-Fidelity Outfit-Level Virtual Try-On with Clothing and Accessories
task: Outfit-level virtual try-on with multiple garments and accessories
model: (diffusion/CNN) SOTA VTON and image-editing models adapted as baselines
inputs: Reference garment images (3–12 per outfit); model image; outfit annotations
outputs: Virtual try-on with correct layering and styling
key_results: Garments2Look: 80K outfit pairs, 40 categories, 300+ subcategories; current methods struggle with layering inference
comments: First multi-garment VTON dataset valuable; synthesis pipeline with filtering/validation ensures quality; baselines expose layering gap.

title: SCoCCA: Multi-modal Sparse Concept Decomposition via Canonical Correlation Analysis
task: Multi-modal concept discovery via sparse CCA-aligned decomposition
model: (CLIP-based) Sparse Concept CCA (SCoCCA) with CCA alignment
inputs: Image-text pairs from vision-language embeddings
outputs: Disentangled concept activations with cross-modal alignment
key_results: SOTA concept discovery; improved activation, ablation, semantic manipulation; sparsity enforces disentanglement
comments: CCA-InfoNCE connection elegant; cross-modal interpretability extension valuable; concept semantics remain implicit.

title: VoXtream2: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking rate control
task: Full-stream TTS with dynamic speaking-rate control mid-utterance
model: (diffusion) VoXtream2 with distribution matching and classifier-free guidance
inputs: Incremental text; audio prompt (optional); desired speaking rate
outputs: Streaming audio with minimal latency and controllable rate
key_results: 4x faster than real-time; 74 ms first-packet latency on consumer GPU; competitive zero-shot quality
comments: Distribution matching for duration control practical; textless prompting removes transcription burden.

title: SNCE: Geometry-Aware Supervision for Scalable Discrete Image Generation
task: Scaling discrete image generation with large VQ codebooks
model: (VQ-VAE + diffusion) Stochastic Neighbor Cross Entropy (SNCE)
inputs: Images; VQ codebook indices with soft neighbor distributions
outputs: Generated images via large-codebook discrete representation
key_results: Improved convergence and generation quality vs. hard cross-entropy; effective on ImageNet-256, text-to-image, editing
comments: Geometry-aware soft targets elegant; neighbor proximity proportionality intuitive; scaling benefits consistent.

title: sebis at ArchEHR-QA 2026: How Much Can You Do Locally? Evaluating Grounded EHR QA on a Single Notebook
task: Privacy-preserving EHR question answering on commodity hardware
model: (transformer) Smaller LLMs optimized for local deployment
inputs: EHR data; clinical questions across four subtasks
outputs: Answers with relevant EHR evidence
key_results: Competitive leaderboard performance without cloud APIs; above-average on two subtasks; feasibility on single notebook
comments: Clinical deployment constraints (privacy, compute) well-addressed; local-only evaluation valuable for regulated domains.

title: Exploring electron spin dynamics in spin chains using defects as a quantum probe
task: Characterize electron spin dynamics and coherence in topological defects of spin chains
model: (theory) dimerized spin chains with topological edge states
inputs: Spin chain dimerization gap, phonon interactions, inter-defect dipolar coupling
outputs: Relaxation rates, coherence times, homogeneous linewidth broadening
key_results: Phonon-bottlenecked relaxation at low T, dimerization-gap-governed relaxation at high T; coherence time longer than isolated ions due to reduced effective dipolar field
comments: Provides practical design principles for quantum devices, though real materials may face additional decoherence sources not captured by the model.
hypotheses: Phonon-bottlenecked process dominates relaxation at lowest temperatures
Chain dimerization gap governs relaxation at high temperature
Intrachain exchange coupling reduces inter-defect dipolar field and extends coherence
results: Confirmed: phonon bottleneck identified at low T
Confirmed: dimerization gap controls high-T relaxation
Confirmed: longer coherence than isolated ions at equivalent concentration

title: Real-time simulation of jet energy loss and entropy production in high-energy scattering with matter
task: Simulate real-time scattering dynamics and energy loss in gauge theories
model: (theory) 1+1D massive QED with tensor network methods
inputs: Probe state, target medium density, collision parameters
outputs: Probe energy loss rate, entanglement entropy evolution, scattering regimes
key_results: Probe energy loss scales linearly with path length; three regimes identified (ballistic, local excitation, black-disk); entanglement entropy reveals probe-medium mixing
comments: Bridging high-energy physics and quantum simulation is promising, but 1+1D setup may not capture full complexity of realistic nuclear collisions.
hypotheses: Three qualitatively distinct dynamical regimes exist based on target density
Probe energy loss rate is linear with path length
Entanglement entropy reveals quantum mechanical mixing between probe and medium
results: Confirmed: ballistic, intermediate, and black-disk regimes observed
Confirmed: linear energy loss scaling with path length
Confirmed: entanglement entropy tracks probe-medium mixing

title: Bose–Hubbard simulator with long-range hopping
task: Realize Bose-Hubbard model with long-range hopping using exciton arrays
model: (experiment) dipolar excitons in nanoscopic lattices
inputs: Exciton density, lattice geometry, dipolar interaction strength
outputs: Radiative dissipation rate, temporal coherence, Mott phase properties
key_results: Long-range hopping evidenced by algebraic slowdown of radiative dissipation; threshold coherence increase in dipolar quantum solids; excitons condense in single sub-radiant state for Mott phases
comments: Novel platform combining dipolar interactions with lattice geometry, though dissipation and finite-size effects may limit observation of long-range correlations.
hypotheses: Long-range hopping produces sub-radiance and slowdown of radiative decay
Dipolar quantum solids exhibit threshold coherence increase
Excitons condense in sub-radiant state for Mott-like phases with spatial order and extended coherence
results: Confirmed: algebraic slowdown of dissipation observed
Confirmed: threshold coherence increase for dipolar solids
Confirmed: single-state condensation in Mott phases

title: Origin of strange metallicity in a d-orbital kagome metal
task: Identify microscopic origin of strange metallicity in kagome metal
model: (experiment) kagome metal Ni₃In with flat band near Fermi energy
inputs: Magnetic field, temperature, scanning tunneling spectroscopy probe
outputs: Zero-bias peak-dip spectral features, quasiparticle properties, orbital localization signature
key_results: Zero-bias peak arises from compact molecular orbitals via destructive quantum interference; orbital-driven localization creates emergent f-shell-like moments in d-orbital metal; quasiparticle collapse across Brillouin zone
comments: STS reveals microscopic mechanism of strange metallicity, but connection to bulk transport properties requires additional measurements.
hypotheses: Zero-bias peak originates from compact molecular orbitals formed by destructive quantum interference
Orbital-driven localization creates emergent localized moments in d-orbital kagome metal
Spectral features track evolution of strange metal state with field and temperature
results: Confirmed: zero-bias peak attributed to interference-derived molecular orbitals
Confirmed: orbital localization generates f-shell-like moments
Confirmed: peak-dip evolution correlates with strange-metal behavior

title: Vertical substitution strategy to enable cooperation between spin–orbit coupling and transition dipoles for organic phosphorescence
task: Enhance organic phosphorescence through cooperative spin-orbit and transition dipole coupling
model: (molecule design) conjugated organic systems with vertical substituents
inputs: Main-group element substituents perpendicular to conjugation plane, molecular geometry
outputs: Phosphorescence efficiency, emission wavelength, intensity enhancement
key_results: Vertical substitution cooperatively enhances spin-orbit and transition dipoles; organic phosphorescence efficiency reaches fluorescence levels across visible wavelengths
comments: Novel molecular design strategy differs from standard horizontal-substituent approach, though quantum yield limits and triplet-state lifetimes merit further study.
hypotheses: Vertical substitution creates cooperative enhancement of spin-orbit coupling and transition dipoles
This cooperation increases phosphorescence efficiency to match fluorescence levels across visible spectrum
results: Confirmed: vertical substitution enhances phosphorescence efficiency
Confirmed: efficiency comparable to fluorescence across visible range

title: A new free surface identification method for 3D MPS method
task: Improve free-surface identification in 3D Moving Particle Semi-Implicit method
model: (numerical method) MPS with relative position divergence (RPD) formulation
inputs: Particle positions, particle number density, normal vector estimation
outputs: Surface particle classification, pressure field predictions
key_results: RPD+NV method reduces false negatives/positives; smoother, more physically consistent pressure fields; no significant computational cost increase; validated on convex, concave, and cavity-containing geometries
comments: Robust surface detection improves MPS accuracy for practical applications, though method still depends on local particle density variations.
hypotheses: Improved RPD formulation achieves better consistency with theoretical values
RPD coupled with surface normal vector (RPD+NV) accurately identifies particles on complex boundaries
Combined method reduces pressure oscillations without computational penalty
results: Confirmed: improved RPD consistency demonstrated
Confirmed: RPD+NV identifies surface particles on convex, concave, and cavity boundaries
Confirmed: pressure oscillations reduced; minimal cost increase

title: Deep learning analysis for enhanced prediction of heat transfer in Maxwell hybrid nanofluids with non-Fourier law and radiation effects
task: Predict heat transfer in Maxwell hybrid nanofluids using deep learning surrogate models
model: (numerical + ML) MATLAB bvp4c solver + multilayer perceptron neural network
inputs: Magnetic parameter (0.1–1.2), radiation parameter (0.3–0.9), Biot number (0–0.8), velocity slip
outputs: Velocity and temperature distributions, velocity gradients, Nusselt number
key_results: ANN achieves MSE ≤ 6.9×10⁻¹² for velocity gradients, ≤ 1.5×10⁻¹¹ for temperature; R ≈ 1 across all subsets; thermal radiation and Biot enhance heat transfer; magnetic field suppresses velocity
comments: ANN surrogate model provides excellent accuracy and computational efficiency, but generalization to out-of-range parameters requires validation.
hypotheses: Thermal radiation and Biot number enhance heat transfer rates
Magnetic field effects suppress velocity field
ANN models accurately capture nonlinear boundary-layer physics with negligible error
results: Confirmed: thermal radiation and Biot increase heat transfer
Confirmed: magnetic parameter suppresses velocity
Confirmed: ANN reproduces numerical solutions with R≈1

title: Temperature control performance change of EPS foam box with ice packing in aircraft cargo hold
task: Evaluate refrigeration performance of foam box with ice packing in aircraft cargo hold
model: (simulation) finite element energy balance and temperature field model
inputs: Low-pressure cargo hold environment, convective heat transfer coefficient, insulation properties
outputs: Temperature distribution inside foam box, cooling rate, ice consumption
key_results: Refrigeration effect degrades in aircraft cargo hold compared to ground; degradation increases with convective heat transfer coefficient; moderate ice increase recommended for air transport
comments: Practical guidance for air cargo operations, though model simplifications and uncertainty in convective coefficients may limit quantitative predictions.
hypotheses: Low-pressure aircraft environment reduces refrigeration effectiveness compared to ground transport
Cooling degradation correlates with convective heat transfer coefficient
results: Confirmed: refrigeration effect changes in aircraft cargo hold
Confirmed: performance degrades with increasing convective heat transfer

title: Deterministic statistical patterns preceding ice shocks revealed by ice deformation measurements
task: Identify short-term precursor patterns preceding ice shock fracture events
model: (statistical analysis) deformation measurement time series with topological pattern recognition
inputs: Ice deformation measurements, thermal expansion history, temporal evolution
outputs: Statistical precursor patterns, temporal signatures, fracture prediction indicators
key_results: Ordered linear features emerge preceding ice shocks; transition from stochastic to deterministic dynamics; precursors appear ~1 hour, ~tens of minutes, and ~minutes before failure; increasing topological similarity indicates progressive synchronization
comments: Statistical approach identifies deterministic precursors in complex fracture process, though applicability to other systems and sensitivity to measurement noise require investigation.
hypotheses: Ice shock approach accompanied by transition from stochastic to deterministic dynamics
Multiple groups of short-term precursors exist at different timescales before failure
Progressive synchronization of deformation processes precedes fracture
results: Confirmed: deterministic patterns emerge before ice shocks
Confirmed: precursor groups identified at ~1 hour, ~tens of minutes, ~minutes timescales
Confirmed: topological similarity increases progressively prior to failure

title: Étendue and radiance conservation in transformation optics establish strict analytical bounds on field enhancement
task: Establish fundamental bounds on field enhancement in passive transformation optics
model: (theory) transformation optics with phase-space formulation and Liouville theorem
inputs: Coordinate transformation, geometric area-compression ratio, impedance-matching condition
outputs: Maximum achievable field enhancement, radiance and étendue conservation constraints
key_results: Maximum average intensity in passive concentrator limited solely by geometric area-compression ratio; Liouville's theorem enforces phase-space volume preservation; constraint applies universally to zero-index, optical-null, and illusion devices
comments: Elegant metric-based framework provides fundamental limits, though practical devices may face additional constraints from dispersion and material losses.
hypotheses: Passive transformation optics performs canonical (symplectic) mapping on optical phase space
Liouville's theorem applies to transformation-optical media, enforcing phase-space conservation
Maximum field enhancement limited by geometric area-compression ratio independent of material realization
results: Confirmed: TO establishes canonical phase-space mappings
Confirmed: phase-space volume preservation enforced
Confirmed: concentration limits depend only on geometric compression ratio

title: Constructing machine learning interatomic potentials with minimum amount of ab initio data
task: Develop system-specific machine learning interatomic potentials with minimal ab initio data
model: (ML) fine-tuned MACE model → distilled NEP model with active sampling
inputs: Few hundred DFT calculations, pretrained MACE model, target system
outputs: Fast, accurate NEP model for large-scale MD; comparison of models and computational efficiency
key_results: Fine-tuned MACE retains stability of pretrained model; single-shot workflow significantly reduces DFT overhead compared to conventional active learning; fine-tuned MACE is ~10× more DFT-efficient than start-from-scratch NEP; final NEP enables competitive accuracy in large-scale MD
comments: Practical workflow leveraging transfer learning and model distillation achieves efficiency gains, though applicability depends on similarity between pretrained MACE data and target system.
hypotheses: Fine-tuning pretrained MACE model is more DFT-efficient than training start-from-scratch NEP
Distilling fine-tuned MACE into NEP maintains accuracy for large-scale MD
Single-shot MACE-driven sampling strategy reduces active learning iterations
results: Confirmed: fine-tuning MACE more efficient than start-from-scratch NEP training
Confirmed: distilled NEP achieves competitive accuracy
Confirmed: single-shot workflow reduces total DFT cost

title: Thermal and dielectric performance of transformer oil-based nanofluid with fullerene C60 nanoparticles
task: Evaluate thermal and dielectric performance of C60-fullerene-doped transformer oil
model: (experiment + analytical) distribution transformer with natural convection loop model
inputs: C60 concentration (0.004% w/V), transformer loading, thermal and electrical test conditions
outputs: Thermal conductivity, AC breakdown voltage, flash point, temperature rise, heat circulation effects
key_results: C60 concentration negligible impact on viscosity/density/thermal conductivity; AC breakdown voltage increased 65%; flash point decreased; transformer met EN 60076-2:2011 standard; slight temperature reduction due to enhanced oil circulation; analytical model predictions agree with experiments
comments: Industrial-scale production and deployment demonstrate practical viability, though long-term aging and particle agglomeration require monitoring.
hypotheses: Very small C60 concentration significantly increases AC breakdown voltage
C60 particles enhance coupling between windings and cooling surfaces
Nanofluid meets transformer safety standards
results: Confirmed: 65% AC breakdown voltage increase at 0.004% concentration
Confirmed: slight temperature reduction from improved circulation
Confirmed: meets EN 60076-2:2011 standard

title: Effect of copper rod length on the melting behavior of paraffin wax in hemispherical latent heat storage units
task: Enhance melting performance of paraffin wax using copper rod inserts in hemispherical geometry
model: (simulation) ANSYS Fluent 16 with enthalpy-porosity method
inputs: Copper rod length (0, 10, 20, 30 mm), RT42 paraffin wax, hemispherical cell geometry
outputs: Melting time, melting rate, temperature distribution, convective currents
key_results: Melting time reduced from 300 min (no rod) to 150, 120, 90 min with 10, 20, 30 mm rods respectively; highest melting time reduction 70%; melting rate increased 233%; stronger convection and more homogeneous temperature distribution
comments: Simple, effective thermal enhancement for organic PCMs, though rod placement optimization and material corrosion/compatibility merit further investigation.
hypotheses: Copper rod length directly enhances thermal conductivity and convection
Increasing rod length monotonically reduces melting time
Rod presence strengthens convective currents and homogenizes temperature
results: Confirmed: 70% melting time reduction with 30 mm rod
Confirmed: monotonic improvement with increasing rod length
Confirmed: enhanced convection and temperature homogeneity

title: Structures of respiratory supercomplexes and ATP synthase oligomers in mammalian mitochondrial inner membrane
task: Determine structures of ATP synthase and respiratory supercomplexes in native mitochondrial membranes
model: cryo-electron microscopy on sub-mitochondrial particles
inputs: Sub-mitochondrial particles (SMPs) from bovine heart mitochondria
outputs: High-resolution 3D structures of FoF1-IF₁ dimers/tetramers and respiratory supercomplex compositions
key_results: FoF1 tetrameric units stabilized by IF₁; identification of new supercomplex composition CI₁CIII₂CIV₃ and mega-complex CI₂CIII₂CIV₆; Fo domain e-subunit–c₈-ring interface resolved without discrete lipid density
comments: This work elegantly bridges structural biology with functional organization of cristae, enabling rapid characterization from minimal membrane fractions.
hypotheses: FoF1 complexes oligomerize via IF₁-mediated interactions to organize membrane architecture
Respiratory complexes assemble into functionally distinct supercomplex stoichiometries
results: Confirmed FoF1 tetramers contribute to cristae tip shaping
Identified additional supercomplex compositions beyond previously known variants

title: Massively parallel quantification of mutational impact on IAPP amyloid formation
task: Map mutational effects on IAPP amyloid nucleation and aggregation
model: deep mutational scanning of 1916 IAPP variants
inputs: Islet amyloid polypeptide sequence variants (substitutions, insertions, truncations, deletions)
outputs: Quantitative mutational atlas showing relative amyloid nucleation propensity for each variant
key_results: Residues 15-32 are highly sensitive to mutation; residues 21-27 show tightest structural constraints; nucleation-slowing mutations correlate between IAPP and Aβ but acceleration mutations do not
comments: The finding that mutation effects are directionally asymmetric between different amyloid peptides suggests context-dependent mechanistic differences in nucleation pathways.
hypotheses: IAPP core region (15-32) is structured in amyloid fibrils and constrains sequence variation
Mutations slowing nucleation share common biophysical principles across different amyloid peptides
Mutations accelerating nucleation exploit peptide-specific sequence context
results: Identified continuous mutation-sensitive region matching experimentally observed early aggregated species
Demonstrated asymmetric correlation of mutational effects between IAPP and Aβ amyloids

title: Kinase KEY1 controls pyrenoid condensate size throughout the cell cycle by disrupting phase separation interactions
task: Elucidate kinase-mediated regulation of biomolecular condensate size and dynamics
model: biophysical model of phase separation with phosphorylation-dependent interaction disruption
inputs: Cell cycle phase; KEY1 kinase activity; Rubisco-EPYC1 linker protein interactions
outputs: Condensate size, number, and positioning dynamics; dissolution and re-condensation kinetics
key_results: KEY1 phosphorylates EPYC1 to disrupt Rubisco-EPYC1 interactions and control pyrenoid condensate size; key1 mutants display multiple persistent small condensates unable to dissolve during cell division; biophysical model predicts condensate position control
comments: This work elegantly connects post-translational modification to phase separation regulation, offering a generalizable mechanism for controlling condensate properties that likely extends beyond pyrenoids.
hypotheses: Kinase-mediated phosphorylation regulates biomolecular condensate size by modulating core-constituent interactions
KEY1 activity is necessary for pyrenoid dissolution and reconfiguration during cell division
Phase separation interaction strength scales with condensate size and positioning
results: KEY1-phosphorylated EPYC1 disrupts Rubisco binding and promotes condensate dissolution
KEY1 activity required for cell cycle-dependent condensate dynamics and normal photosynthetic function

title: Calcium-mediated calreticulin-IRE1α interaction drives dynamic fluctuation of IRE1α activity under chronic endoplasmic reticulum stress
task: Characterize UPR dynamics under chronic ER stress
model: (experiment) pharmacologically induced ER stress in cultured cells and mouse liver
inputs: Long-term ER stress (tunicamycin/thapsigargin); ER calcium levels
outputs: IRE1α phosphorylation; calreticulin-IRE1α interaction intensity; ER calcium dynamics
key_results: IRE1α activity shows 'up-down-up' fluctuation pattern; calreticulin-IRE1α interaction negatively correlates with Ca2+ concentration and exhibits circadian oscillation in mouse liver
comments: Elegant mapping of temporal dynamics reveals calcium-dependent regulation that may explain why chronic ER stress can be both pro-survival and pro-apoptotic.
hypotheses: Calreticulin-IRE1α interaction inversely correlates with ER calcium
This interaction oscillates with circadian rhythm in vivo
results: Confirmed; 'down-up-down' Ca2+ pattern drives 'up-down-up' IRE1α activity
Confirmed in mouse liver with regular physiological oscillations

title: Polar chromosomes are rescued from missegregation by spindle elongation-driven microtubule pivoting
task: Elucidate mechanism of polar chromosome rescue from missegregation
model: (experiment) live-cell imaging; Eg5/KIF11 modulation in cultured cells
inputs: Polar chromosomes initially behind spindle pole; spindle elongation signals
outputs: Chromosome positioning; microtubule pivoting angle and extent; kinetochore attachment topology
key_results: Spindle elongation drives astral microtubule pivoting around centrosome; polar chromosomes form mono-lateral kinetochore attachments lacking Astrin; enhanced elongation accelerates polar chromosome resolution
comments: Mechanistic insight into a common source of chromosomal instability in cancer; the 'danger zone' concept provides a useful framework for understanding segregation errors.
hypotheses: Spindle elongation repositions astral microtubules via pivoting
Polar chromosomes require distinct kinetochore architecture for rescue
results: Confirmed; microtubule pivoting directed by Eg5-dependent elongation
Confirmed; mono-lateral attachments and missing Astrin distinguish polar chromosomes

title: Phosphoregulation of the novel hemi-arrestin MAPK scaffold Sms1 prevents untimely mating
task: Identify MAPK scaffold regulating sexual differentiation signaling in fission yeast
model: (experiment) fission yeast (S. pombe) mating pathway
inputs: Pheromone activation; PIP2 membrane localization signals
outputs: Sms1 membrane translocation; MAPK cascade assembly and activation; mating pathway output
key_results: Sms1 (hemi-arrestin) recruits MAPK cascade to membrane via Gα and PIP2; phosphorylation (including by MAPK itself) antagonizes membrane localization, creating negative feedback; prevents untimely mating
comments: Convergent evolution of MAPK scaffolds despite distinct structural folds highlights functional convergence; the feedback mechanism elegantly explains how cells prevent spurious mating attempts.
hypotheses: Sms1 acts as essential MAPK scaffold for mating pathway
MAPK-mediated phosphorylation of Sms1 creates negative feedback
results: Confirmed; Sms1 assembles active cascade at membrane patches
Confirmed; feedback antagonizes membrane translocation and prevents signal overamplification

title: β-tubulin phosphorylation by Chk1 is required for normal spindle formation during cell division
task: Determine role of Chk1-mediated β-tubulin phosphorylation in spindle formation
model: (experiment) vertebrate cell culture (HeLa, RPE1); in vitro kinase assays
inputs: β-tubulin; Chk1 kinase; ATR-ATRIP-TopBP1 signaling complex at centrosomes
outputs: β-tubulin-T285 phosphorylation; spindle microtubule density and nucleation; chromosome segregation fidelity
key_results: Chk1 phosphorylates β-tubulin at T285 at centrosomes; impaired phosphorylation causes improper spindle, delayed anaphase, chromosome misalignment, unequal daughter cell size; ATR-ATRIP-TopBP1 promotes Chk1 activation
comments: Surprising discovery that a DNA damage kinase (Chk1) functions in unperturbed mitosis; suggests spindle integrity checkpoints share kinase machinery with DNA damage responses.
hypotheses: Chk1 phosphorylates β-tubulin at conserved T285 residue
This phosphorylation is essential for proper spindle assembly and function
results: Confirmed in vitro and in vivo at mitotic centrosomes
Confirmed; T285 phosphorylation deficiency causes multiple spindle and chromosome segregation errors

title: Next-generation platelet concentrate: Alb-PRF outperformed PRP and H-PRF in promoting endothelial function and wound healing
task: Compare efficacy of albumin-cross-linked PRF vs. conventional platelet concentrates for angiogenesis

key_results: Alb-PRF superior to PRP and H-PRF in promoting HUVEC proliferation and migration; Alb-PRF rescues LPS-induced apoptosis and upregulates CD31; superior angiogenic capacity under normal and inflammatory conditions
comments: Clinical translation appears promising, but lacks in vivo wound healing validation and mechanistic insight into why albumin cross-linking provides advantage over conventional PRF formulations.
hypotheses: Alb-PRF promotes superior HUVEC proliferation vs. PRP/H-PRF
Alb-PRF provides cytoprotection against inflammatory stress
results: Confirmed; higher viability and migration rates observed
Confirmed; LPS-induced apoptosis rescued only by Alb-PRF

title: A selective and augmentable butyrate-FFAR2 signal circuitry programs the cellular identity of enteroendocrine L-cells
task: Elucidate butyrate-FFAR2 signaling in L-cell GLP-1/PYY secretion
model: (experiment) human L-cell model; mouse intestinal organoid (Hes1-GFP reporter)

outputs: GLP-1/PYY secretion ratio; NeuroD1 and Pax6 expression; Hes1 signal; transcriptional profile

comments: Demonstrates how dietary fiber metabolites fine-tune enteroendocrine cell identity; selective Gαi-biased agonism offers potential therapeutic avenue for obesity without classical β-arrestin signaling.

This suppresses Hes1-mediated anti-secretory Notch signaling
results: Confirmed; Gαi-dependent without dynamin internalization
Confirmed; Notch modulation in organoid model and upregulation of secretory maturation factors

title: Kynurenine promotes angiogenesis through mTOR signaling in head and neck squamous cell carcinoma
task: Determine kynurenine-mTOR signaling axis in HNSCC angiogenesis
model: (experiment) HNSCC tissue; HUVECs; CAM assay; CDX mouse model
inputs: Kynurenine (tryptophan metabolite); IDO1 enzyme expression; endothelial cells
outputs: HUVEC migration and tube formation; neovascularization; mTOR activation; angiogenic gene expression
key_results: IDO1-driven kynurenine elevated in HNSCC correlates with CD31/CD34 endothelial markers; kynurenine enhances HUVEC migration, tube formation and CAM neovascularization; activates mTOR signaling upregulating pro-angiogenic genes (VEGF, bFGF, IL-8)
comments: Links tryptophan catabolism in tumor microenvironment to vascular remodeling; suggests metabolic reprogramming actively shapes angiogenesis, offering druggable targets beyond anti-angiogenic ligand inhibition.
hypotheses: Kynurenine promotes endothelial migration and tube formation
This occurs via mTOR pathway activation
results: Confirmed in vitro and in CAM model
Confirmed; mTOR cascade upregulates angiogenic mediators

title: Androgen receptor localisation and protein interactions provide insight into steroid mediated metabolic shifts in endocrine resistant breast cancer
task: Characterize cytoplasmic AR localization and metabolic reprogramming in AI-resistant breast cancer
model: (experiment) AI-resistant breast cancer cell lines; human tumor microarray; LC-MS/MS proteomics
inputs: Adrenal androgens; aromatase inhibitor-resistant conditions; AI-sensitive vs. resistant cells
outputs: AR subcellular localization; interacting protein partners; mitochondrial mass/potential; oxidative metabolism
key_results: High cytoplasmic AR predicts poor survival in luminal B post-menopausal breast cancer; AI-resistant cells show diffuse cytoplasmic AR with increased mitochondrial mass, oxidative phosphorylation and glycolysis; G3BP1, SLIRP, IGFBP5 identified as AR-interacting proteins mediating metabolic adaptation and ERα repression
comments: Novel mechanistic link between non-genomic AR signaling and metabolic rewiring in endocrine resistance; cytoplasmic AR localization may be a biomarker for treatment-refractory disease with prognostic value.
hypotheses: Cytoplasmic AR localizes in AI-resistant cells
Cytoplasmic AR interactome drives metabolic adaptation
results: Confirmed; high cytoplasmic AR associated with poor survival
Confirmed; G3BP1/SLIRP/IGFBP5 interactions linked to metabolic shifts and ERα suppression

title: Glutamate decarboxylase 1 (GAD1) suppresses the progression of glioblastoma through GSK3β/β-catenin pathway
task: Elucidate GAD1/GABA pathway suppression of glioblastoma via Wnt-signaling inhibition
model: (experiment) glioblastoma cell lines (U87, LN229); zebrafish xenotransplantation
inputs: GAD1 overexpression/knockdown; GSK3β inhibitor AR-A014418
outputs: Cell proliferation; colony formation; cell cycle phase distribution; invasion; p-GSK3β/β-catenin signaling; Cyclin D1, MMP9 expression
key_results: GAD1 downregulated in glioma correlating with poor prognosis; overexpression suppresses proliferation, invasion, and cell cycle progression via reduced p-GSK3β/β-catenin signaling; GSK3β inhibitor phenocopies GAD1 knockdown effects; zebrafish model confirms tumor suppression
comments: Interesting connection between neuronal GABA synthesis enzyme and canonical Wnt pathway suppression; raises questions about whether GAD1 functions as a metabolic sensor or whether GABA itself mediates effects.
hypotheses: GAD1 suppresses Wnt signaling via GSK3β/β-catenin axis
This inhibits Cyclin D1 and MMP9 driving tumor progression
results: Confirmed; GAD1 reduces p-GSK3β and β-catenin levels
Confirmed in cell lines and in vivo zebrafish model

title: SIRT1 induces FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4-induced ferroptosis and accelerates the progression of cerebral infarction by inhibiting the E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP signaling pathway
task: Determine SIRT1-mediated ferroptosis pathway in cerebral infarction
model: (experiment) rat MCAO/reperfusion model; primary astrocytes; bioinformatic mining of ferroptosis-related genes
inputs: SIRT1 activation/inhibition; E2F1, NOTCH-1, YAP signaling; ferroptosis triggers
outputs: Infarction volume; astrocyte apoptosis; autophagy markers (Beclin-2, P62); ferroptosis markers (MDA, Fe2+, SOD); FOXO1 and NCOA4 expression
key_results: SIRT1 upregulated in cerebral infarction; SIRT1 activation increases infarct volume and astrocyte apoptosis; SIRT1 inhibits nuclear E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP while upregulating FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4 cascade; increased MDA and Fe2+ accumulation; NOTCH-1 overexpression reverses effects
comments: Mechanistically complex with multiple pathway intersections; the linkage between SIRT1, ferroptosis and NOTCH signaling is novel but would benefit from clearer cause-effect ordering and validation of ferroptosis vs. apoptosis as primary death mechanism.
hypotheses: SIRT1 activation induces ferroptosis in cerebral infarction
This occurs via E2F1/NOTCH-1/YAP suppression and FOXO1/autophagy/NCOA4 induction
results: Confirmed; SIRT1 upregulation correlates with infarct progression
Confirmed; SIRT1 alters pathway expression and ferroptosis markers

title: The MIME-seq technique allows to monitor the interaction of small non-coding RNAs with Argonaute proteins and their transfer to other cells
task: Develop sequencing technique to monitor sncRNA-Argonaute interactions and cell-cell transfer
model: (experiment) MIME-seq2.0 with engineered methyltransferase-Argonaute chimera; MIN6B1, Jurkat, C2C12 cell lines
inputs: Small non-coding RNAs (miRNAs, Y-RNAs, tRNA-fragments); extracellular vesicles; Argonaute proteins
outputs: Protected (non-oxidized) sncRNA abundance; sncRNA-Ago2 binding status; transferred miRNA detection in recipient cells
key_results: MIME enzyme protects oxidized sncRNAs from degradation enabling detection; distinguishes Ago2-bound miRNAs, Y-RNAs, and tRNA-fragments; successfully tracks miRNA transfer between Jurkat/C2C12 cells and MIN6B1 recipients via EVs
comments: Clever technical solution to a real problem (sncRNA oxidation preventing cloning), but the biological novelty is limited; applications to mechanistic questions about EV miRNA function remain to be demonstrated.
hypotheses: MIME enzyme protects oxidized sncRNAs from degradation
Protected sncRNAs enable sensitive EV miRNA transfer tracking
results: Confirmed; protected Y-RNA and tRNA-fragments are Ago2-bound
Confirmed; miRNAs in EVs remain protected and detectable in recipient cells

title: Hydroxychloroquine alleviates cyclophosphamide-induced premature ovarian failure by attenuating granulosa cell senescence and modulating the mtDNA-cGAS pathway
task: Elucidate hydroxychloroquine protection against CTX-induced premature ovarian failure via mtDNA-cGAS pathway
model: (experiment) CTX-induced POF mouse model; human granulosa cells (KGN); phosphoramide mustard (CTX metabolite)
inputs: Hydroxychloroquine (HCQ); cyclophosphamide; mtDNA and ROS signals
outputs: Follicle number; serum FSH/estradiol; granulosa cell senescence markers; mtDNA leakage; cGAS-STING activation; SASP factors
key_results: HCQ reverses CTX-induced ovarian dysfunction and follicular depletion; attenuates senescence, stabilizes mitochondrial membranes, decreases ROS and mtDNA leakage, inhibits cGAS-STING signaling and SASP expression; improves fertility outcomes
comments: Well-designed preclinical study with clear mechanistic pathway (mitochondrial dysfunction → mtDNA leak → cGAS activation → senescence); HCQ's anti-autophagy effects appear paradoxically beneficial here, suggesting context-dependent roles.
hypotheses: HCQ prevents CTX-induced mtDNA leakage and ROS production
This suppresses mtDNA-cGAS-STING-driven senescence and SASP
results: Confirmed; HCQ stabilizes mitochondrial membranes and decreases mtDNA/ROS
Confirmed; cGAS-STING inhibition and reduced senescence markers and SASP factors

title: Systematic background selection with BasCoD enhances contrastive dimension reduction in single cell genomics
task: Develop statistical framework for selecting appropriate background datasets in contrastive dimension reduction
model: (computational) spectral subspace inclusion theory; contrastive dimension reduction methods
inputs: Target dataset; multiple candidate background datasets; single-cell genomic data
outputs: Background suitability score; optimized target representation; contrast enrichment metrics
key_results: BasCoD framework based on spectral subspace inclusion theory enables rigorous background selection; improves contrast and interpretability of target representations across diverse single-cell datasets; guides experimental design for large-scale perturbation studies
comments: Addresses real practical problem in single-cell analysis where background choice critically impacts downstream interpretation; statistical rigor is strong but biological validation of selected backgrounds is limited.
hypotheses: Spectral subspace inclusion theory predicts suitable background datasets
Systematic background selection improves contrastive representation quality
results: Confirmed; BasCoD identifies backgrounds yielding high contrast
Confirmed; improved target representation interpretability across datasets

title: Molecular basis of oocyte cytoplasmic lattice assembly
task: Determine molecular architecture of oocyte cytoplasmic lattice assembly
model: (structural) cryo-EM of CPL isolated from mouse oocytes; protein-protein interaction mapping
inputs: Oocyte cytoplasmic lattice components; CPL-containing oocyte lysates
outputs: CPL three-dimensional structure; subunit composition; interaction interfaces; assembly organization
key_results: CPL comprises 14 constitutive proteins organized as repeating 'U-shaped basket' (UB) and 'adapter ring' (AR) units forming filamentous architecture; PADI6 forms didecameric anchor; AR contains NLRP4f, SCMC, ZBED3; UBE2D3-UHRF1-NLRP14 and TUBB2B-TUBB2A-FBXW24-SKP1 complexes form UB sides
comments: Landmark structural study resolving a long-standing mystery about a critical but poorly understood structure; provides molecular basis for understanding oocyte maturation and early embryonic development, though functional validation of specific interaction interfaces remains ongoing work.
hypotheses: CPL forms periodic filamentous architecture with repeating structural units
Specific protein-protein interaction networks maintain CPL integrity
results: Confirmed; cryo-EM structure reveals UB-AR repeat organization
Confirmed; identified 14 subunits and critical interaction clusters maintaining structure

title: BTG3 suppresses oral squamous cell carcinoma progression by inhibiting PI3K/AKT signaling and EMT
task: Identify BTG3 tumor suppressor function in oral squamous cell carcinoma
model: (experiment) OSCC cell lines (CAL27, SCC15); GEO/TCGA database analysis; gain/loss-of-function studies

This suppresses OSCC proliferation and EMT progression
results: Confirmed; BTG3 reduces pAKT levels

title: VISDB 2.0: A manually curated resource of viral integration sites and their regulatory maps in human diseases
task: Catalog viral integration sites and their genomic associations
model: N/A

key_results: 3.5-fold increase in VIS entries; standardized annotations across retroviruses and DNA viruses; web-based query interface

title: Local lateral connectivity is sufficient for replicating cortex-like topographical organization in deep neural networks
task: Investigate emergence of topographic cortical organization in neural networks

inputs: Visual input data; lateral recurrent connections between neighboring units

Lateral recurrence improves adversarial robustness
results: Topographic maps emerge without explicit topography induction
Enhanced robustness to adversarial perturbations observed

title: An attention-based multimodal deep learning framework integrating EEG and ECG for enhanced stress detection
task: Multimodal stress detection from EEG and ECG signals

key_results: 95.7% accuracy (vs. 82.3% EEG-only, 85.6% ECG-only); significant synergy between modalities; 26.9% inference speedup
comments: Strong empirical results on two datasets, but cross-dataset generalization and real-world deployment feasibility warrant further investigation.
hypotheses: Multimodal fusion outperforms unimodal stress detection
Attention-based fusion captures complementary signal information
results: 95.7% three-way classification accuracy achieved
EEG and ECG modalities enhance each other's predictive power

title: In silico discovery of thioglycoside analogues as donor-site inhibitors of glycosyltransferase LgtC
task: Identify glycosyltransferase LgtC inhibitors targeting UDP-galactose donor pocket

comments: Rigorous computational screening with MD validation, but in vitro biochemical assays remain necessary to confirm donor-site inhibition.

Stable hydrogen-bond and π-stacking interactions maintained in MD simulations

title: Zero-shot English–Assamese neural machine translation via pivot-based cross-lingual embedding alignment and transfer learning
task: Zero-shot English–Assamese translation via cross-lingual embedding alignment

Phenotypic traits alone can predict yield

Cotyledon morphology strongly predicts total wet bean mass

task: Characterize structure–function relationships of fatty acid ethanolamides and ROS-mediated apoptosis

inputs: FAE chain length; physicochemical properties (solubility, hydrophobicity); cell-based assays (ROS, MMP, apoptosis)
outputs: Acyl chain length as dominant determinant; apoptotic activity profiles; docking poses with cannabinoid receptors
key_results: C12:0-MEA shows distinct ROS/MMP/apoptosis profile; C16:0–C18:0-MEA exhibit enhanced hydrophobicity and apoptosis; chain length explains ~70% variance (PCA)
comments: Comprehensive structure–function study with elegant PCA synthesis, but the split between ROS-dependent and independent pathways in long-chain FAEs requires mechanistic clarification.
hypotheses: Acyl chain length is the dominant physicochemical determinant
C12:0-MEA exhibits optimized bioactivity for targeted prodrug applications
results: Chain length explains dominant variance in physicochemical and biological behavior
C12:0-MEA shows controlled bioactivity profile; long-chain FAEs activate both ROS-dependent and independent pathways

title: Automated detection and segmentation of Weiss ring in fundus photography images using deep learning
task: Automated detection and segmentation of Weiss rings in fundus photographs
model: (experiment) U-Net segmentation + EfficientNet-B0 classification + meta-classifier fusion
inputs: Fundus photography images; segmentation and classification targets
outputs: Weiss ring localization masks; binary classification (presence/absence); Grad-CAM attention maps
key_results: U-Net Dice 0.578, IoU 0.421; meta-classifier AUC 0.903, accuracy 0.812, sensitivity 0.692, specificity 0.872
comments: Modest segmentation performance due to low contrast and peripapillary visibility challenges; clinical utility limited to archival review and research phenotyping without OCT confirmation.
hypotheses: Segmentation and classification fusion outperforms classification alone
Attention maps localize Weiss ring appearance patterns
results: Meta-classifier outperforms individual CNNs (AUC 0.903 vs. unimodal)
Grad-CAM highlights peripapillary regions of Weiss ring appearance

title: Origins and breadth of pairwise epistasis in an α-helix of β-lactamase TEM-1
task: Characterize mechanistic basis of epistasis in protein mutations
model: Two-state protein stability model with additive contributions
inputs: Library of 14,000+ double mutants in TEM-1 β-lactamase α-helix
outputs: Fitness, minimum inhibitory concentration, epistatic interaction predictions
key_results: Most epistasis predictable from single-mutation effects via stability model; systematic deviations occur for residues in direct 3D contact; statistical models from homologous sequences partially recover epistatic interactions
comments: The finding that structure-dependent interactions explain deviations from additivity provides mechanistic insight, though the model's predictive power outside this specific helix remains unclear.
hypotheses: Epistatic effects arise primarily from changes in protein stability
Direct residue-residue interactions in 3D structure cause non-additive epistasis
Evolutionary information from homologous sequences encodes epistatic patterns
results: Two-state model explains most epistatic variance
Direct interactions identified as source of model deviations
Homolog-trained statistical models partially predict observed epistasis

title: Biosynthesis of pyomelanin from methanol with engineered Komagataella phaffii and its characterizations
task: Engineer high-yield pyomelanin biosynthesis from methanol
model: (Experiment) Engineered Komagataella phaffii with balanced biosynthetic modules and directed evolution
inputs: Methanol, engineered biosynthetic pathways (homogentisic acid synthesis and oxidative polymerization)
outputs: Pyomelanin titer (g/L), homogentisic acid yield fold-change
key_results: 66-fold increase in homogentisic acid yield; strain Pyo29 achieved 70.5 ± 0.7 g/L pyomelanin in 204 h fermentation; established color-screening system for directed evolution
comments: The substantial yield improvement demonstrates practical pathway engineering, though the balance between the three biosynthetic modules and their individual contributions could be better characterized.
hypotheses: Balancing biosynthetic modules increases flux through homogentisic acid synthesis
Directed evolution of 3-deoxy-D-arabinoheptulosonate 7-phosphate synthase improves upstream efficiency
Laccase-catalyzed polymerization differs mechanistically from alkaline oxidative polymerization
results: Module balancing achieved 66-fold homogentisic acid yield increase
Directed evolution and semirational design improved enzyme efficiency
High-density fermentation reached 70.5 g/L pyomelanin titer
